entity.name: House Energy and Commerce Committee
entity.description: Primary House committee for AI legislation touching consumer protection, telecom, data privacy, and FTC oversight. Hosts the Innovation, Data, and Commerce subcommittee where federal preemption fights (APRA, AI provisions) play out.

activity.title: Subcommittee on Commerce, Manufacturing, and Trade Hearing: Computing Power and Competition: Examining the Semiconductor Ecosystem
activity.raw_text: WASHINGTON, D.C. – Today, Congressman Brett Guthrie (KY-02), Chairman of the House Committee on Energy and Commerce, and Congressman Gus Bilirakis (FL-12), Chairman of the Subcommittee on Commerce, Manufacturing, and Trade, announced a hearing titled Computing Power and Competition: Examining the Semiconductor Ecosystem. "Semiconductors are foundational to modern life, powering everything from cars, computers, and phones to basic household appliances like toasters and refrigerators, along with critical tools essential for our national defense. Vulnerabilities in the semiconductor supply chain underscore the need for robust oversight of the semiconductor ecosystem"

_verify_phrases: Computing Power and Competition: Examining the Semiconductor Ecosystem
